active_opportunities.subject: Pittsburgh International Airport Autonomous Vehicle (AV) Shuttle Project
how_to_comment: Mail: PennDOT P3 Office, 400 North St., 6th Floor, Harrisburg PA 17120
active_opportunities.description: ACAA's proposed AV shuttle system connecting parking facilities to the new landside terminal at Pittsburgh International Airport via dedicated guideway.

active_opportunities.agency: Allegheny County Health Department (ACHD) — Air Quality
active_opportunities.subject: Industrial air quality permit(s)
active_opportunities.location: ACHD Air Quality, 836 Fulton Street, Pittsburgh, PA 15233
active_opportunities.registration: Required at least 24 hours in advance via ACHD Public Hearing Participation Form
active_opportunities.notes: Check achd public comment page for specific permit subject and deadline

active_opportunities.type: Regional Body Board Meeting with Public Comment
active_opportunities.subject: Regular Board Meeting — Clean Water Plan, capital projects, GROW grants
how_to_comment: In-person: Register at Administration Building before meeting

active_opportunities.type: Regional Body Committee Meeting (Open to Public)
active_opportunities.subject: GROW (Green Revitalization of Our Waterways) stormwater grant program — Cycle 12/13 reviews and recommendations
Transportation Improvement Program (TIP) and Long-Range Transportation Plan
active_opportunities.notes: Most recent TIP public comment round wrapped December 2025. New rounds triggered by plan amendments or updates. Active Transportation Forum (quarterly) open to public.

active_opportunities.agency: PA House and Senate Appropriations Committees
active_opportunities.subject: FY 2026-27 Executive Budget proposed by Gov. Shapiro (February 3, 2026)
active_opportunities.notes: PA Chamber recap: hearings week of March 9-13, 2026. Live coverage via PCNTV. Public can submit written comments to their legislators.
active_opportunities.pittsburgh_items: PRT transit funding, housing $1B bond fund, Municipalities Planning Code modernization

active_opportunities.subject: Air quality regulation amendments (Chapter 121/127) — general permits framework
active_opportunities.notes: DEP revising draft proposed regulation on air quality general permits (addressing Act 45 statutory provision). Will be presented to advisory committees in Q2 2026 before public comment period.

active_opportunities.subject: Active and upcoming projects near Pittsburgh
active_opportunities.notes: District 11 covers Allegheny, Beaver, and Lawrence Counties. Project-specific public meetings posted per project.

active_opportunities.subject: Housing affordability hearing
active_opportunities.notes: Example of Western PA legislative hearings. Chaired by Sen. Nick Miller. Featured Senators Fontana, Williams, Costa, Pisciottano.

active_opportunities.subject: SmartMoves Long-Range Transportation Plan and 2025-28 TIP overview

active_opportunities.notes: EngagePGH (engage.pittsburghpa.gov) is the City of Pittsburgh's public engagement platform — already tracked per task instructions. Current open items include Housing Needs Assessment (HNA) zoning bill implementation: ADUs, inclusionary zoning, transit-oriented development, parking reform, Fair Housing amendments.

commissions.name: Pittsburgh Land Bank
commissions.how_to_comment: Public comment is accepted at the beginning of each meeting (immediately after roll call) and again at the end of the meeting. Members may register to give live comment via the form at pghlandbank.org or submit written comment to staff@pghlandbank.org. Registration closes at 11:00 AM the day of the meeting.

next_meeting.notes: Second Friday of March 2026 — registration required via pghlandbank.org

commissions.meeting_schedule: Monthly, second Friday of each month at 1:00 PM. No meeting in January 2025. 2026 schedule not yet posted as of March 2026.

commissions.name: Pittsburgh Art Commission (Public Art & Civic Design Commission — PACD)
commissions.how_to_comment: Public comment is accepted during Hearing and Action items. Attendees may comment in-person or via Zoom (raise hand function). Email pacd@pittsburghpa.gov to receive agendas.

next_meeting.format: Hybrid: Lower Level Hearing Room, 412 Blvd of the Allies + Zoom

commissions.meeting_schedule: Fourth Wednesday of each month at 2:00 PM (December recess — no meeting in December).

commissions.name: Pittsburgh Historic Review Commission (HRC)
commissions.how_to_comment: Public testimony accepted for Hearing and Action items. Can be provided in-person, via Zoom (raise hand), by phone (press *9), by email to historicreview@pittsburghpa.gov (by noon the business day before the meeting), or by letter (must arrive 2 business days before the meeting).

next_meeting.format: Hybrid: Lower Level Hearing Room, 412 Blvd of the Allies + Zoom

commissions.meeting_schedule: First Wednesday of each month at 1:00 PM (runs until 5:00 PM). January meeting starts at 12:00 PM.

commissions.name: City of Pittsburgh Equal Opportunity Review Commission (EORC)
commissions.how_to_comment: At the meeting, the Commission Chair calls for public testimony for each agenda item. In-person testimony at Room 646, 414 Grant Street. Remote testimony via Zoom. Pre-registration by 9:00 AM the day of the meeting requested. EORC Meeting Public Comment Registration Form available on the website. Phone: 412-255-8804.

next_meeting.format: Hybrid: Room 646, City County Building + Zoom

commissions.meeting_schedule: Monthly at 12:00 PM (noon). 2026 dates: January 15, February 19, March 19, April 16, May 21, June 18, and continuing monthly through the year.

commissions.name: Pittsburgh Civil Service Commission
commissions.how_to_comment: Public meetings and public hearings are held. All public meeting and hearing minutes are public documents available by contacting the Secretary and Chief Examiner at 412-255-2042.

next_meeting.notes: Every Thursday — exact dates estimated based on weekly Thursday schedule

commissions.meeting_schedule: Every Thursday at 8:00 AM. Meeting dates and times are posted at the Department of Personnel and Civil Service Commission.

commissions.name: Pittsburgh Commission on Human Relations (CHR)
commissions.how_to_comment: Commission meetings are open to the public. Public comment accepted at each meeting. Meetings begin at 3:30 PM.

next_meeting.format: Hybrid: Room 902, City County Building + Zoom + YouTube Livestream

commissions.meeting_schedule: Monthly, first Monday of each month at 3:30 PM. (Pattern based on 2025 meeting dates: Jan 6, Feb 3, Mar 3, Apr 7, May 5, Jun 2, Jul 7, Sep 8, Oct 6, Nov 3, Dec 1.)

commissions.name: Pittsburgh Ethics Hearing Board (EHB)
commissions.how_to_comment: Meetings are open to the public. The Ethics Hearing Board meets as needed for public meetings or executive sessions. Contact the Ethics Office for details on participating.

next_meeting.format: Hybrid: Conference Room 902, City County Building + Video conference

commissions.meeting_schedule: Monthly, first Thursday of each month at 4:00 PM. Public meeting times are reserved monthly; times may be used for public meetings or for non-public executive sessions. 2025 dates: 1/9, 2/6, 3/4, 4/3, 5/1, 6/5, 7/10, 8/7, 9/4, 10/2, 11/6, 12/4. 2026 dates confirmed include: 3/5, 5/7 (from City Planning Calendar which also shows Ethics Hearing Board listings), 11/5, 12/3.

commissions.name: Sports & Exhibition Authority of Pittsburgh and Allegheny County (SEA)
commissions.how_to_comment: Public participation at open meetings is a statutory right. Members of the public may provide comments by email prior to the meeting, by telephone, or via Zoom chat (when applicable). Public participation is the first and last item on the agenda.

next_meeting.notes: Second Thursday of March 2026 — estimated based on pattern; confirm at pgh-sea.com

commissions.meeting_schedule: Monthly, typically the second Thursday of each month at 10:30 AM. Cancelled meetings occur occasionally (e.g., January 2025 and August 2025 were cancelled).

commissions.name: Stadium Authority of the City of Pittsburgh
commissions.how_to_comment: Public participation at open meetings is a statutory right accorded to residents and taxpayers of Allegheny County. Contact 412-393-0200 for minutes or questions.

next_meeting.notes: Confirmed — notice posted at pgh-sea.com and Pittsburgh Post-Gazette classifieds

commissions.meeting_schedule: Quarterly (approximately 4 times per year). Confirmed 2026 meeting dates: March 26, 2026; June 10, 2026; September 17, 2026; December date TBD.

commissions.name: Pittsburgh-Allegheny County Task Force on Disabilities (City-County Task Force on Disability — CCTFD)
commissions.how_to_comment: Meetings are open to the public. Subscribe to meeting notifications via CCTFDNews listserv. Contact: ada@pittsburghpa.gov.
commissions.meeting_schedule: Monthly, third Monday of each month at 2:00 PM (except holidays). 2026 confirmed CCTFD meeting dates: March 16 (Virtual), April 20 (Virtual), May 18 (Virtual), June 15, July 20, August 17, September 28, October 19, November 16, December 21.

commissions.name: Pittsburgh Citizens Police Review Board (CPRB)
commissions.how_to_comment: Meetings are open to the public. All meetings start at 6:00 PM. Public may attend via Zoom or watch the YouTube livestream.
commissions.meeting_schedule: Monthly, last Tuesday of each month at 6:00 PM. 2026 confirmed dates: February 24; March 24; April 28; May 26 (from 2026 proposed calendar). January 2026 was cancelled. No meeting in August (summer recess).
